seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Boulder and South Burracoppin. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Boulder vs South Burracoppin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Boulder and South Burracoppin.
heading: South Boulder vs South Burracoppin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Boulder, Western Australia and South Burracoppin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Burracoppin has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to South Boulder (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Boulder or South Burracoppin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Boulder has a flood risk score of 55/100, while South Burracoppin scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Boulder scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. South Burracoppin scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Boulder or South Burracoppin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Burracoppin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
